prerequisites: A FireHydrant account and organization at app.firehydrant.io.
Owner permissions on the organization, which are required to create API keys.
An API token generated in FireHydrant (this is the only required config field, api_token).

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: FireHydrant uses token-based authentication. The connector authenticates with the api_token secret field, which is sent as an Authorization: Bearer {token} header on every request to the FireHydrant API.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to FireHydrant with Owner permissions. Go to Settings > API Keys, click Create API key, enter a name and description (for reference only), and click Save. The token value is displayed only once, so copy it immediately.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the copied token into the connector's required api_token field. The connector sends it as Authorization: Bearer {token} against the FireHydrant API base URL https://api.firehydrant.io/v1.

setupSteps.title: Verify access
setupSteps.body: Confirm the credential works by running the connection check. The API key inherits the permissions of the account that created it (Owner by default), so make sure that account can read the resources you intend to sync.

sources.title: FireHydrant Documentation - List permissions (shows base URL https://api.firehydrant.io/v1)
